channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: THE KRUGER NATIONAL PARK HIT: Why Mutare Has Become the Secret Hideout for Cross-Border Murder Suspects
item.description: The geography of Crooks’ Corner is as treacherous as its history. Located at the extreme northern tip of South Africa’s Kruger National Park, it is the precise point where the borders of South Africa, Zimbabwe, and Mozambique converge. For decades, this confluence of the Limpopo and Luvuvhu rivers was a lawless sanctuary for ivory poachers [...] The post THE KRUGER NATIONAL PARK HIT: Why Mutare Has Become the Secret Hideout for Cross-Border Murder Suspects first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Equatorial Guinea government resigns due to missed targets, VP says
item.description: Equatorial Guinea’s government resigned on Tuesday after failing to meet its objectives, the West African oil-producing country’s Vice President ​Teodoro Nguema Obiang Mangue said. President Teodoro Obiang Nguema Mbasogo appointed ​Manuel Osa Nsue Nsua as prime minister ⁠in 2024. The president’s son and the country’s vice ​president, Teodoro Nguema Obiang Mangue, said on X that [...] The post Equatorial Guinea government resigns due to missed targets, VP says appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Argentina’s Messi equals World Cup goalscoring record with hat-trick in defeat of Algeria
item.description: Lionel Messi equalled Miroslav Klose’s all-time record for goals at World Cup tournaments when he netted for the 16th time in Argentina’s 3-0 opening win over Algeria on Tuesday. Messi, playing at a record sixth World Cup, netted a spectacular opener from distance in Kansas City, then poked home from close range before completing his [...] The post Argentina’s Messi equals World Cup goalscoring record with hat-trick in defeat of Algeria appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Eaglesvale VS CBC Schools Rugby chaos FULL DETAILS: Zipra army commander Nikita Mangena’s son arrested for bashing pupil & producing a gun
item.description: HARARE – The final whistle at a schools rugby match is usually a moment of either jubilation or respectful defeat. However, this past Saturday at Eaglesvale College in Harare, the conclusion of a high-stakes fixture against Bulawayo’s Christian Brothers College (CBC) descended into a scene of unprecedented disorder. What began as a sporting rivalry ended [...] The post Eaglesvale VS CBC Schools Rugby chaos FULL DETAILS: Zipra army commander Nikita Mangena’s son arrested for bashing pupil & producing a gun first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: How Modern Gaming Blurs the Line Between Hobby and Lifestyle
item.description: Have you ever planned to play for a little while, then realized gaming had become part of how you relax, connect, and express yourself? Modern gaming is no longer just something people do when they have spare time. For many players, it fits naturally into daily life, like music, fitness, cooking, reading, or watching shows. [...] The post How Modern Gaming Blurs the Line Between Hobby and Lifestyle first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Stanbic Boosts Growth and Development in Agricultrure @263Chat
item.description: Leading financial services institution, Stanbic Bank Zimbabwe is committed to play a pivotal role in fostering the growth and development of the agricultural value chain. The Standard Bank Group subsidiary has made significant strides in providing both medium- and long-term financing solutions tailored to the unique needs [...] The post Stanbic Boosts Growth and Development in Agricultrure appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Every student to write Zimsec exams: Cambridge University and Ministry of Education fight as government bans Cambridge alternative
item.description: HARARE – A high-stakes diplomatic and educational row has erupted between the Zimbabwean government and Cambridge University Press & Assessment, following a direct challenge to the country’s new mandatory schooling policy. The clash, which has left thousands of parents and private schools in a state of nervous anticipation, centres on whether elite private institutions can [...] The post Every student to write Zimsec exams: Cambridge University and Ministry of Education fight as government bans Cambridge alternative first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Double murder convict receives two life sentences @bulawayo24
item.description: A 37-year-old man from Chipinge has been sentenced to two life terms by the High Court in Mutare after being convicted of murdering two girlfriends in separate incidents committed more than two years... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Zimbabwe blockchain platform unveiled @bulawayo24
item.description: The Financial Securities Exchange (FINSEC) has completed the development and testing of a blockchain infrastructure platform that will underpin Zimbabwe’s emerging market for tokenised assets, marking... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: GMAZ Retreats from Grain Levy Battle, Clearing Way for Import Framework Implementation
item.description: The Grain Millers Association of Zimbabwe (GMAZ), chaired by Tafadzwa Musarara, has withdrawn its High Court application challenging Government’s grain import levy framework introduced under Statutory Instrument 87 of 2025. According to a Notice of Withdrawal filed at the High Court Commercial Division in Harare on June 15, 2026, GMAZ withdrew its application and tendered [...] The post GMAZ Retreats from Grain Levy Battle, Clearing Way for Import Framework Implementation appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.description: Gun Drama at School Rugby Match Lands Harare Man in Court — via 263Chat #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263Chat

item.title: Liberation Hero’s Son Arrested After Gun And Assault Allegations At School Rugby Match
item.description: Eaglesvale-CBC Rugby Match Turns Chaotic As Parent Faces Gun Charges A Harare parent who is the son of late national hero Alfred Nikita Mangena has been arrested and charged following a violent incident that unfolded after a schools rugby match between Eaglesvale College and Christian Brothers College (CBC) on Saturday, 13 June 2026. Lotshe Yuri [...] The post Liberation Hero’s Son Arrested After Gun And Assault Allegations At School Rugby Match appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Musarara Withdraws Legal Bid to Stop Grain Import Levy Framework @263Chat @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: The Grain Millers Association of Zimbabwe (GMAZ), chaired by suspended ZANU-PF Mazowe District Coordinating Committee (DCC) chairman Tafadzwa Musarara, has withdrawn its High Court application challenging Government’s grain import levy framework introduced under Statutory Instrument 87 of 2025. According to a Notice of Withdrawal filed [...] The post Musarara Withdraws Legal Bid to Stop Grain Import Levy Framework appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Ramaphosa urges South Africans to stop blaming migrants for local issues @CyrilRamaphosa
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa on Tuesday urged South Africans not to blame migrants for the country’s problems, after a recent wave of anti-immigrant unrest and violence. One of Africa’s largest economies, South Africa has long attracted migrant workers from across the continent, both legally and illegally. But in recent weeks, mobs of South Africans carrying sticks, [...] The post Ramaphosa urges South Africans to stop blaming migrants for local issues appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: AI cameras from these 20 different spots in Harare catch 15,000 traffic offending drivers: Here is how you will be notified & how to pay fines
item.description: The Silent Sentinels: How AI Cameras Are Rewriting the Rules of Harare’s Roads HARARE – The era of the “cat and mouse” game between motorists and traffic police on the streets of Harare is coming to a swift, digital end. In a massive technological shift that has caught thousands of drivers off guard, a new [...] The post AI cameras from these 20 different spots in Harare catch 15,000 traffic offending drivers: Here is how you will be notified & how to pay fines first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
